cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the dance project aims to do. The choices are A to D. First, I need to recall the details from the text. The passage talks about LaMar Baylor teaching dance to street boys in Rwanda. The project is by the Rebecca Davis Dance Company. The key points mentioned are that the children have lost their families, some have been in prison or sold their bodies. The dance classes give them structured learning and self-expression they haven't had before. Rebecca Davis believes dance is a step toward education, allowing them to take IT classes after learning to attend classes and follow directions. Some boys get scholarships to Sunrise Boarding School. So the main goal here seems to be more than just dance. The project is using dance as a way to help them get into an educational environment. The passage says dance classes help them learn how to behave in a classroom, which is part of their education. Also, after learning dance, they can take other classes. The scholarships are a result of doing well, but the primary aim isn't just scholarships. The answer should be about education broadly. Looking at the options: A. parental care – the text says the kids have lost families, but the project isn't providing parental care. So probably not A.
